piko_article.title: Stardew Valley Save File Location
strengths: Keeps risky actions behind backup warnings.
Separates platform paths from troubleshooting steps.
limits: This benchmark draft uses a controlled PCGamingWiki/MediaWiki source sample plus a small manual comparison set.
It should stay a draft until a future round verifies page-level evidence and platform-specific edge cases.

materials.title: PCGamingWiki - Stardew Valley
materials.covers: Structured save game data locations for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
materials.notes: Strong structured source, but the raw wiki format is not written as a player-first troubleshooting checklist.

materials.title: Stardew Valley Wiki - Saves
materials.covers: Save transfer and troubleshooting guidance, including missing-save recovery context.
materials.notes: Useful for recovery context, but long and broad for a player who only needs the local save path first.

materials.title: Steam Community discussion - save file location
materials.covers: Short player answers pointing to %AppData%/StardewValley/Saves on Windows.
materials.notes: Direct and practical, but less complete and less structured than a source-backed guide.

takeaways: Piko's draft is strongest as a short answer and risk-filtered checklist.
PCGamingWiki is strong on structured paths, but its raw page format is not always player-friendly.
Official/community wiki material can add context, but Piko should avoid copying tables or long prose.
Next round should verify save folder paths against at least one official/community-maintained source before publishing.
next_recommendation: Keep as draft benchmark and add page-level source verification before any publishing eligibility step.
